title: Shell's $16.4B Bet on Canadian Gas: What It Means for Maryland Energy Bills

title: What's New for Maryland BEPS in 2026: The HB 49 Changes Every Building Owner Must Know
excerpt: House Bill 49 brought significant updates to Maryland BEPS in 2026 — a new $100 annual reporting fee, a full waiver for Montgomery County buildings, and new exemption pathways for hospitals and SCIFs. Here's what changed and what it means for your property.

title: Maryland BEPS Explained: What Building Owners Must Do Before the Deadlines Hit
excerpt: Maryland's Building Energy Performance Standards are now in effect for buildings 35,000 sq ft and larger. Here's what owners need to do before the deadlines hit — and how to use EmPOWER Maryland rebates to fund the path to compliance.

title: Maryland BEPS Third-Party Verification: The Every-Five-Years Rule Building Owners Can't Miss
excerpt: Maryland BEPS requires independent third-party verification of benchmarking reports every five years, starting with 2025 data due June 1, 2026. Here's who qualifies as a verifier, what gets audited, and how to prepare before the deadline.
